steps.title: Identify what the goal requires
steps.content: The student wants to "emphasize the order in which the two lighthouse keepers began their careers." The correct choice must explicitly show which keeper started first and which started later — not when careers ended, not general praise, not navigation impact.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, keep the one that shows career-start order
steps.content:
Check each choice against the goal:
  • Choice A — From 1867 to 1918, the nighttime waters of Mississippi were more navigable thanks to lighthouse keepers Helen Barrow and Clara Whitmore.: focuses on navigation impact and misattributes Barrow to Mississippi — does not establish start order. ✗
  • Choice B — Before automation, lighthouse keepers like Clara Whitmore and Helen Barrow were crucial to ensuring safe navigation for watercraft.: makes a general statement about both keepers; does not compare when either began. ✗
  • Choice C — Helen Barrow began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Clara Whitmore did.: directly states that Whitmore started first (1867) and Barrow started later (1904), establishing the chronological order of career starts. ✓
  • Choice D — Clara Whitmore's career as a lighthouse keeper ended in 1918, whereas Helen Barrow's ended in 1920.: compares when careers ended, not when they began. ✗
That matches Choice C — Helen Barrow began her career as a lighthouse keeper years after Clara Whitmore did..
